_note: ORIGINAL content. javascript.info and Eloquent JavaScript informed topic coverage only; no prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Algorithm Problems: Part 1
summary: Arrange setup, repetition, decisions, and output into a method.
refresher: Name the input and expected output, trace a small example, initialize needed state, perform each update, and return after the work is complete.

blocks.body: Name the input and expected output, trace a small example, initialize needed state, perform each update, and return after the work is complete. Arrange setup, repetition, decisions, and output into a method.

For a small algorithm, translate the prompt into four questions: what comes in, what must come out, what state must be remembered, and what work repeats? A running total remembers progress across iterations. Its starting value should behave neutrally for the operation, which is zero for addition.

blocks.caption: Zero initializes the accumulator, each value contributes once, and return follows the loop.
Do not return an accumulator before traversal finishes.

blocks.body: With `[3, 5, 2]`, total changes from zero to three, then eight, then ten. Return belongs after those three updates. Try an empty array too: the loop makes no updates, and the initialized total of zero is still a sensible sum.

blocks.title: Trace this carefully
blocks.body: Putting return inside the loop ends the function during its first pass and skips the remaining values.

blocks.why: The update needs an existing number, and zero is the correct additive starting point.

blocks.prompt: Where should a full-array maximum return?
blocks.why: Every needed comparison must finish because a later element can replace the candidate.
